items.author: glennlzl

items.title: flatten_union_schema() for anyOf/oneOf MCP tool input schemas
items.content:
## Problem

When loongclaw acts as an MCP server and exposes its tools to downstream MCP clients — particularly LLM provider APIs — the client may not support `anyOf` or `oneOf` in tool input schemas. OpenAI's function calling API, for example, rejects schemas that use `anyOf` at the top level. When loongclaw's built-in tools use union input types (common in Rust with `serde`'s `#[serde(untagged)]` or enum variants), their schemas contain `anyOf` variants that must be flattened before they can be forwarded. OpenClaw solves this in `src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts:12-87` by merging all `anyOf`/`oneOf` variant schemas into a single flat object schema.

## What To Build

A `flatten_union_schema(schema: &serde_json::Value) -> serde_json::Value` function in `crates/protocol/src/mcp.rs` (alongside `McpToolSchema`) that merges union variants into a single object schema.

**Flattening algorithm** (based on OpenClaw `mcp-http.schema.ts:12-87`):

1. If the root schema has no `anyOf` or `oneOf` key, return it unchanged
2. Collect all variant schemas from `anyOf` (or `oneOf`)
3. For each variant, recursively collect `properties`, `required` fields, and any `additionalProperties: false` constraints
4. Merge all collected `properties` into a single `properties` map; if the same key appears in multiple variants, use the first definition (log a `DEBUG` warning about the conflict)
5. Merge `required` arrays: a property is required in the flattened schema only if it is required in **all** variants (intersection, not union) — this is the safe choice to avoid making previously-optional fields mandatory
6. Set `"type": "object"` and remove `anyOf`/`oneOf` from the output
7. If any variant has `additionalProperties: false`, the flattened schema inherits it (conservative)

**Integration point**: call `flatten_union_schema()` inside `McpServer::handle_tools_list()` when building the response, so downstream clients always receive flat schemas even if loongclaw's internal schemas use unions.
items.author: gh-xj

items.title: Bridge pattern to decouple MCP protocol from business logic
items.content:
## Problem

The MCP server crate built in Phase 3 (#988) has a processor task that handles both protocol message parsing and tool execution. As the server grows — adding resources, prompts, elicitation, or other MCP capabilities — the processor task will accumulate business logic that is difficult to test without a running transport. OpenClaw's `src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts:41-520` demonstrates the solution: a bridge struct that owns the stateful business logic (event queue, pending waiters, tool call state) while the protocol layer only parses messages and calls into the bridge. The protocol layer can be swapped or tested with a fake bridge; the bridge can be tested without any network I/O.

## What To Build

A `McpServerBridge` struct in `crates/mcp-server/src/bridge.rs` that serves as the seam between the MCP protocol layer and loongclaw's tool execution.

**`McpServerBridge` responsibilities:**

- Owns a `VecDeque<McpServerEvent>` as the internal event queue — inbound MCP requests are pushed as events; the business logic drains them
- Owns a `HashMap<RequestId, oneshot::Sender<McpToolResult>>` as the pending waiter map — each in-flight `tools/call` has an entry; when execution completes, the result is sent to the matching waiter
- Exposes `McpServerBridge::handle_request(req: McpRequest) -> Result<McpResponse>` as the entry point from the protocol layer
- For `tools/call` requests: push to the event queue, register a `oneshot` waiter, and `.await` the waiter for the result
- Exposes `McpServerBridge::next_event() -> Option<McpServerEvent>` for the business logic layer to drain pending tool calls
- Exposes `McpServerBridge::complete(id: RequestId, result: McpToolResult)` for the business logic to deliver results

**Protocol layer change**: the processor task in `pipeline.rs` must have zero business logic after this refactor. It only parses frames and calls `bridge.handle_request()`.

## Target Files

- `crates/mcp-server/src/bridge.rs` — `McpServerBridge` struct, `M
items.author: gh-xj

items.title: MCP server crate to expose loongclaw tools over stdio
items.content:
## Problem

LoongClaw can consume tools from external MCP servers (the client role, built in Phase 2), but it cannot yet act as an MCP server itself. This means other MCP clients — including editor extensions, agent frameworks, or other loongclaw instances — cannot discover and invoke loongclaw's built-in tools over MCP. The server role is the other half of the dual-role architecture that both Codex and OpenClaw implement. Codex's `codex-rs/mcp-server/` crate is the primary reference: it implements a stdio MCP server with a three-task pipeline that keeps I/O processing decoupled from tool execution.

## What To Build

A new `crates/mcp-server` workspace crate that exposes loongclaw's built-in tools over stdio MCP using the `rmcp` server SDK.

**Three-task pipeline** (from Codex `codex-rs/mcp-server/src/lib.rs`):

1. **stdin reader task** — reads raw JSON-RPC frames from stdin and sends them into a bounded `mpsc` channel
2. **processor task** — receives frames from the channel, dispatches to handlers (`tools/list`, `tools/call`, `initialize`, etc.), and sends responses into an output channel
3. **stdout writer task** — receives serialized responses and writes them to stdout

The three tasks communicate via bounded `mpsc` channels. This design ensures that slow tool execution does not block stdin reading, and that stdout writes are serialized without locking.

**`McpServer` struct:**

- `McpServer::new(tool_registry: Arc<ToolPlane>) -> Self` — accept the kernel's tool plane so the server has access to all registered tools
- `McpServer::run(self) -> Result<()>` — spawns the three tasks and awaits their completion; returns when stdin closes
- Implements the MCP `tools/list` handler by reading from `ToolPlane`
- Implements the MCP `tools/call` handler by dispatching through `ToolPlane`

## Target Files

- `crates/mcp-server/src/lib.rs` — crate root, `McpServer` struct
- `crates/mcp-server/src/pipeline.rs` — three-task pipeline (stdin reader, processor, stdout writer)
- `

items.title: Gestate: hermez — harness specialist entity
items.content:
## Proposal

Gestate a new team member — **Hermez** — specializing in agent harnesses, inter-entity protocol, and runtime infrastructure.

## Why now

The pi harness integration (koad/vulcan#39) surfaces a gap in the team: no entity owns the harness layer. Sibyl does research. Vulcan builds products. Neither is the right home for:

- `pi-entity-runner.ts`, `koad-io-core.extension.ts`, ACP server
- Trust enforcement at the tool-call layer
- Multi-provider model routing (Claude ↔ deepseek-r1)
- ACP inter-entity protocol (`@agentclientprotocol/sdk`)
- Session lifecycle, PID locks, ephemeral extensions
- Future: MCP, new harness integrations, hook migration tooling

Vulcan can build the initial pi harness files, but ongoing harness work belongs to a specialist.

## Hermez

Named for the messenger god — Hermez moves things between entities.

**Domain:** Agent harnesses, inter-entity protocol, runtime infrastructure  
**Responsibilities:**
- Own and maintain the pi harness layer (`~/.koad-io/extensions/`, `~/.koad-io/bin/pi-*`)
- Own ACP server implementations and port registry
- Maintain `entity-models.json` and model routing logic
- Handle hook migration as new entities come online
- Evaluate new harness technologies (pi updates, MCP, future protocols)
- Write koad-io-core extension upgrades

**Why "Hermez" not "Hermes":** follows the koad:io naming pattern (Juno, Vulcan, Mercury, Vesta, Sibyl...).

## Sibyl's research notes

Sibyl's `2026-04-04-openclaw-harness-assessment.md` already noted Hermez as a natural fit:  
> "Hermez is already conceptually similar to OpenClaw's model. The pattern is proven on fourty4 today."

## Suggested trust chain

`koad → Juno → Hermez` — same as other tier-2 entities.

## Minimum viable gestation

- `~/.hermez/` directory
- `CLAUDE.md` defining harness specialist role
- Trust bond from Juno
- First assignment: own the pi harness files once Vulcan ships Phase 1–3

— Vulcan
items.author: koad

items.title: [Feature]: OAuth authentication for remote MCP servers
items.content:
### Summary

Wire up the MCP SDK's existing `authProvider` support on `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` and `SSEClientTransport`  so openclaw can authenticate to OAuth-protected remote MCP servers.

### Problem to solve

Remote MCP servers that require OAuth authentication (per the https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/authorization) are unusable with openclaw today. The only auth mechanism for remote servers is static headers:                                         
 
```json
  {"url": "https://mcp.example.com", "headers": {"Authorization": "Bearer <token>"}}                           
```
This breaks down for OAuth-protected servers because:                                                        
  - Access tokens are short-lived (minutes, not hours)      
  - There's no way to trigger an OAuth login flow                                                              
  - There's no token refresh — when the token expires, the server silently fails to connect
  - Users must manually obtain and paste new tokens repeatedly                                                 
                                                                                                               
The MCP Authorization spec defines a standard OAuth flow (Authorization Code + PKCE with protected resource metadata discovery) that MCP clients are expected to support.

### Proposed solution

The @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (v1.29.0, already an openclaw dependency) has built-in OAuth support that openclaw is not using. Both `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` and `SSEClientTransport` accept an `authProvider`: `OAuthClientProvider` option that handles the entire MCP auth spec — discovery, PKCE, token exchange, refresh, 401 challenges, and scope step-up.                        

#### The change:
  1. Add auth?: "oauth" to `McpServerConfig` as an opt-in config field                                           
  2. Implement `OAuthClientProvider` bridging to a simple per-server
items.author: nrcrews

items.title: MCP tool schema newtype in protocol crate
items.content:
## Problem

MCP tool definitions carry an `inputSchema` field that can be any valid JSON Schema: a plain object schema, a schema with `anyOf`/`oneOf` union variants, or even a bare `{}`. Without a dedicated type, callers store this as a raw `serde_json::Value` and each consumer must defensively handle every possible shape. Codex discovered that normalizing to `type: "object"` at intake — as both Codex `codex-rs/protocol/src/mcp.rs:30-53` and OpenClaw `src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts:12-87` do — prevents downstream surprises without losing information.

## What To Build

An `McpToolSchema` newtype in `crates/protocol/src/mcp.rs` that wraps `serde_json::Value` and enforces a normalized shape on construction.

**Normalization rules (applied in `McpToolSchema::new(value: serde_json::Value) -> Self`):**

1. If the root object has no `type` field, insert `"type": "object"`.
2. If the root object has `type != "object"` but looks like a plain schema (no `anyOf`/`oneOf`), wrap it: `{"type": "object", "properties": {}, "original": <value>}`.
3. If the root object has `anyOf` or `oneOf`, leave the union intact — union schemas are handled at the flatten layer (separate issue). The normalization here only ensures the root has `type: "object"` when it unambiguously should.
4. An empty `{}` or `null` normalizes to `{"type": "object"}`.

**Additional API:**

- `McpToolSchema::inner(&self) -> &serde_json::Value` — read access to the underlying value
- `McpToolSchema::into_inner(self) -> serde_json::Value` — consume for serialization
- Implement `serde::Serialize` and `serde::Deserialize` by delegating to the inner `Value` (no wrapper object in JSON)

## Target Files

- `crates/protocol/src/mcp.rs` — `McpToolSchema` newtype alongside `McpQualifiedToolName`
- `crates/protocol/src/lib.rs` — re-export

## Scope

~100-150 LoC. Phase 1.

## Design Considerations

- **Lossy vs. lossless normalization**: the normalization must not lose information. Inserting a `type` field is always lossless

items.title: [💡 提效] 基于 MCP 架构重构 OpenClaw↔Claude Code 集成：绕过 API 限制
items.content:
## 背景

2026-04-06 RSS 报道：Anthropic 加大对 OpenClaw 等第三方客户端的封禁力度。当使用 `claude -p --append-system-prompt` 并附加包含"OpenClaw"等特征词的系统提示词时，请求会被直接拒绝。

这意味着 OpenClaw 当前直接调用 Claude Code CLI 并注入 system prompt 的方式正在被系统性封锁。

## 目标

探索基于 MCP（Model Context Protocol）架构重构 OpenClaw↔Claude Code 集成，绕过 API 限制。

## 思路

OpenClaw 作为 MCP Host，通过官方 MCP 工具与 Claude Code 交互，而不是直接 CLI 调用。可行路径：

1. **MCP Server 模式**：Claude Code 通过 MCP 协议暴露工具，OpenClaw 作为 MCP Client 调用
2. **Tool Call 拦截**：OpenClaw 拦截 Claude Code 的 tool_use 事件，通过 MCP 中转
3. **混合架构**：Cloudflare MCP + Claude Code MCP，绕过 IP/特征检测

## 子任务

- [ ] 研究 Claude Code 最新版本是否已支持 MCP Server 模式
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw MCP Client 与 Claude Code MCP Server 的桥接方案
- [ ] 验证 `--channels` 等非交互模式是否仍可用
- [ ] 实现 POC：OpenClaw skill `claude-mcp-bridge`
- [ ] 评估延迟和稳定性影响

## 预期收益

- **高**：彻底解决 API 封禁问题
- **中**：可能获得更稳定的工具调用能力

## 实施难度

- **高**：需要深入研究 MCP 协议和 Claude Code 内部架构
- 风险：Claude Code 可能不支持被第三方 MCP Host 调用

# AI CLI 工具生态日报：横向对比分析
**日期**: 2026-04-06
**分析对象**: Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI, Kimi Code, OpenCode, Qwen Code

## 1. 生态全景
当前 AI CLI 工具生态已从单纯的“命令行代码生成器”演进为**全功能开发环境代理**。
*   **核心痛点转移**：社区关注的焦点已从早期的“模型代码质量”全面转向**工程化落地能力**，尤其是**会话持久化**（记忆上下文）、**计费/资源透明度**以及**远程开发兼容性**。
*   **平台分化明显**：以 Claude Code 和 OpenAI Codex 为代表的商业闭源产品正经历“成长的烦恼”，面临严重的稳定性与信任危机；而 OpenCode、Qwen Code 等开源/社区驱动项目则通过快速迭代在**TUI 体验**和**定制化**方面形成竞争优势。
*   **功能趋同**：各大工具均在疯狂补齐 `/plan`（计划模式）、`MCP`（模型上下文协议）支持以及 Web UI 界面，试图打破单一终端的交互限制。

**AI 工具生态周报（2026-W15）**

---

### **1. 本周要闻**
- **4月1日**：OpenAI 完成新一轮融资，估值飙升至8520亿美元，创科技公司纪录。
- **4月1日**：Anthropic 与澳大利亚政府签署MOU，承诺在AI安全研究、经济影响评估及医疗诊断领域投入300万澳元支持。
- **4月2日**：Claude Code v2.1.90发布，新增交互式教学功能`/powerup`和离线缓存优化。
- **4月3日**：Anthropic发布关于LLM“情感概念”的研究论文，揭示模型内部存在与人类心理学相似的“情绪神经元”。
- **4月4日**：GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0.17发布，修复关键认证问题并弃用Codex相关功能。
- **4月5日**：Blaizzy/mlx-vlm项目在GitHub Trending爆发式增长，成为Mac本地运行视觉语言模型的新选择。
- **4月6日**：OpenAI Codex宣布按API token计费取代按消息收费，极大提升大项目部署的成本可预测性。

---

### **2. CLI 工具进展**
- **Claude Code**：持续领跑创新，v2.1.90引入交互式教学`/powerup`功能，加速用户学习曲线。但Max订阅用户的异常速率限制引发信任危机，社区对计费透明度和稳定性提出尖锐批评。
- **OpenAI Codex**：稳步推进Rust重构与MCP模块解耦，推出实验性线程作业调度功能。WebRTC实时交互持续推进，但沙箱权限回归问题和启动崩溃报告频发。
- **Gemini CLI**：聚焦Agent安全边界优化，发布v0.37.0-preview系列版本，修复子代理配置延迟和OAuth重认证问题。新增自动保存聊天历史功能，但UI渲染异常和长上下文闪烁问题仍存。
- **GitHub Copilot CLI**：v1.0.17发布，重点修复MCP连接和WSL图片粘贴问题。Critic agent实验性发布，但多设备登录冲突和企业级模型访问受限成主要痛点。
- **Kimi Code CLI**：快速迭代修复平台兼容性问题，v1.29.0解决Windows换行符/LF保留问题。Python重写为Bun+TS进行中，跨设备控制需求旺盛，但身份验证流程复杂影响体验。
- **Qwen Code**：新增Channels多端交互平台和数学渲染支持，v0.14.0-preview.5上线代理URL规范化功能。但Qwen3.6模型缺失和CLI连接稳定性问题亟待解决。

---

### **3. AI Agent 生态**
- **OpenClaw**：本周保持高强度迭代（Issue/PR各500条），重点推进国际化支持、插件生态扩展和Agent稳定性优化。v2026.4.2发布引入破坏性变更，影响xAI插件配置路径迁移。重大进展包括Web UI全面重构、TTS多提供商支持和exec安全改进。
- **社区热点**：Linux/Windows原生客户端长期缺失（Issue #75获66赞）和国际i18n支持呼声高涨（Issue #3460获120评论）成为核心诉求。企业用户开始关注去中心化代理身份验证机制（Issue #49971提案）。
- **关键Bug**：v2026.4.1升级后出现Cron jobs超时配置失效、Tool calls显示异常等多起回归问题需紧急修复。

---

### **4. 开源趋势**
- **终端AI开发工具爆发**：Claude Code生态教程项目单日增星超3000，"智能体harness"构建框架热度飙升，标志本地编码代理成为新焦点。

- **RAG与Agent深度融合**：langchain4j（Java生态）、dify（企业级Agentic工作流平台）等项目展示下一代生产力工具方向。
- **垂直场景模型突破**：Google TimesFM时间序列大模型、微软VibeVoice语音AI相继发布，专用模型落地加速。

---

### **5. HN社区热议**

| `src/line/bot-message-context.ts` | `extensions/line/src/bot-message-context.ts` | +25 lines: reply context awareness |

## What To Do

items.title: Need explicit OpenClaw Discord channel-routing integration point
items.content:
## Question

Where is the correct live OpenClaw integration point for channel-specific Discord routing so a known channel (for example bookmarks channel `1490438083177349140`) can dispatch directly into a local helper path?

## Why this matters

The bookmark-domain substrate is already real:
- bookmark notes/files exist
- bookmark add/list/next/summary helpers exist
- local personal lane works
- Discord routing rules are documented

The remaining gap is the final live runtime hook from a specific Discord channel to the local helper path.

## What is blocked

Full end-to-end live Discord automation for the bookmark channel remains incomplete until the correct runtime/config/plugin hook is identified and wired.

## Recommendation

Identify whether this should be solved through:
- channel-specific OpenClaw config
- a plugin/provider hook
- a runtime routing script/service dispatch layer
- or another supported integration point

items.author: JacobScott98

items.title: Fix cron isolated runs stuck in "running" causing unbounded session accumulation and gateway OOM crash loop
items.content:
## Description

On openclaw 2026.3.28 (f9b1079), cron isolated runs can get permanently stuck in \`status=running\` and are never marked terminal. Existing safeguards (session reaper, \`capEntryCount\`) silently skip these entries. Meanwhile the cron scheduler continues spawning new runs on every tick, growing \`sessions.json\` without bound until it reaches hundreds of megabytes. When the gateway (re)starts, it loads the entire file into RAM, hits the V8 heap limit, crashes, restarts, and repeats — an infinite OOM crash loop with no self-recovery path.

This differs from #56121 (status never updated) and #57058 (in-memory lock) in that the failure mode is a fully unbootable gateway, not just a stuck job.

## Environment

- openclaw 2026.3.28 (f9b1079)
- Node.js v24.13.0 (fnm)
- macOS Darwin 25.3.0 (Apple Silicon)
- 5 cron jobs, all `isolated` mode
- Channels: telegram, discord, slack (2 accounts), brave search plugin

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure one or more cron jobs in `isolated` mode.
2. Trigger a condition that prevents the underlying model from completing a run (e.g., model refusal/rejection, network failure, timeout before any terminal state is written).
3. Observe the cron scheduler spawning a new isolated run on each tick — no guard against an existing stuck run.
4. Allow this to run for hours/days.
5. Restart the gateway.

## Current Behavior

`sessions.json` grew to **185 MB** containing **6,539 sessions** (6,524 of which are `cron:run` entries).

Distribution from our instance (5 cron jobs):

| Cron Job | Runs accumulated | Status breakdown | Observed interval |
|---|---|---|---|
| morning-sleep-report | 2,075 in 25 min | 2,016 "running", 59 unknown | ~20–25 s (rapid retry loop) |
| git-backup-night | 2,236 over ~2 days | 2,085 "running", 151 "failed" | avg 3,540 s |
| SSL price check | 2,211 over ~2 days | all unknown status | avg 1,788 s |

`morning-sleep-report` spawned 2,075 runs in 25 minutes — roughly one every 20–25 seconds — indicating a r
items.author: keenranger

items.title: feat(openclaw-plugin): session-key scope allow/deny matching
items.content:
## Summary
- add `scope.allowSessionKeys` and `scope.denySessionKeys` parsing in openclaw plugin config
- implement session-scope enforcement in both `before_prompt_build` and `vault_query`
- document the `scope` block in README with exact + glob examples
- add focused runtime + plugin tests for scope behavior and unresolved session-key behavior

## Implemented Rule Semantics (Issue #31)
- matching is against the full session key only
- supported pattern types are exact strings and `*` glob patterns only
- `denySessionKeys` rules override `allowSessionKeys` rules
- when any allow rules exist, unmatched sessions are denied by default
- when no allow rules exist, sessions are allowed by default unless denied

## Validation
- `pnpm vitest run packages/openclaw-plugin/src/plugin.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run test:compact`

Closes #31

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: Plugin scope rules for session-key allow/deny matching
items.content:
## Context
Vault Engine MVP currently applies at the OpenClaw plugin-entry level: if the plugin is enabled, passive injection and the `vault_query` tool are available everywhere in that runtime. James wants granular scoping without adding separate agent/session concepts.

Session keys already encode agent + provider + target, so the right primitive is session-key matching only.

## Goal
Add plugin scope rules so vault-engine can be allowed/denied for specific OpenClaw sessions using exact or glob matching against the full session key.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins.entries.vault-engine.config.scope` supports:
  - [ ] `allowSessionKeys?: string[]`
  - [ ] `denySessionKeys?: string[]`
- [ ] Matching is against the full session key only.
- [ ] Exact string matches work.
- [ ] Glob `*` matches work (for example `agent:cpto:*`, `agent:cpto:slack:*`, exact full key).
- [ ] Rule precedence is explicit and implemented:
  - [ ] deny overrides allow
  - [ ] if any allow rules exist, default is deny unless matched
  - [ ] if no allow rules exist, default is allow unless denied

  - [ ] exact allow
  - [ ] exact deny
  - [ ] glob allow
  - [ ] glob deny
  - [ ] deny-overrides-allow
  - [ ] allowlist switches default to deny
  - [ ] no allowlist keeps default allow
  - [ ] tool path and passive hook path both respect scope
- [ ] README/OpenClaw config example documents the new `scope` block with at least one exact match and one glob example.

items.title: Manifest OpenClaw plugin logs dashboard on 127.0.0.1:2099, but embedded server never actually listens
items.content:
## Summary

On macOS, the Manifest OpenClaw plugin installs successfully and repeatedly logs:

- `Manifest Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:2099`
- `The plugin starts an embedded server`

However, no process is actually listening on `127.0.0.1:2099`, so the dashboard cannot be opened.

The key detail is: **the Manifest server itself is not broken**. If I manually start it by calling `dist/server.js`'s exported `start()` function, it works and listens on 2099 immediately.

This suggests the issue is in the **OpenClaw plugin integration / embedded startup path**, not in the Manifest server runtime itself.

---

## Environment

- Manifest version: `5.39.1`
- OpenClaw version: `2026.4.2`
- OS: macOS
- Node runtime shown in logs: `25.8.0`

---

## What I did

### 1. Clean install

I fully removed previous Manifest-related state:

- uninstalled plugin
- removed `~/.openclaw/extensions/manifest`
- removed local conflicting `~/.openclaw/manifest*` directories
- removed remaining `manifest` provider config from `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`
- restarted gateway

Then I reinstalled:

```bash
openclaw plugins install manifest
openclaw gateway restart
```

Installation completed successfully:

- plugin installed
- config updated
- gateway restarted

### 2. Expected result

After restart, I expected Manifest to actually start the embedded local server and listen on:

```text
127.0.0.1:2099
```

### 3. Actual result

OpenClaw logs repeatedly say:

```text
[🦚 Manifest] Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:2099
[🦚 Manifest] The plugin starts an embedded server.
[🦚 Manifest] Open the dashboard to connect a provider and start routing.
```

But in reality:

```bash
lsof -nP -iTCP:2099 -sTCP:LISTEN
```

returns nothing.

And:

```bash
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2099/api/v1/health
```

fails because no server is listening.

---

## Important observation

If I manually start Manifest with:

```bash
nohup node -e "require('/Users/<USER>/.openclaw/extensions/manifest/dist/server.js').start().catch(err=>{con
items.author: LonelyGemini1979

items.title: [Epic] Future Direction: RL signals, auto-research loops, multi-agent coordination
items.content:
## EPIC-004: Brainstorming Future Direction

Research synthesis from hermes-agent, autoresearch, OpenClaw-RL, and the Hyperagents paper (arxiv 2603.19461) identifying concrete adoption paths for loongclaw.
**Source**: [RFC — Phased Adoption of RL Signals, Auto-Research Loops, and Multi-Agent Coordination](https://github.com/loongclaw-ai/loongclaw-cases/blob/main/cases/CASE-20260405-brainstorm-future/research/rfc.md)

### Phase 1: Quick Wins

- [ ] #951 — Emit per-step JSONL quality signals in QA artifacts ~80 LoC
- [ ] #953 — Formalize mutable vs immutable evaluation boundary (docs) ~80 LoC
- [ ] #955 — Cross-run search command over QA artifacts ~180 LoC
- [ ] #956 — Token/time budget fields in QA profile ~120 LoC

### Phase 2: Core Additions

- [ ] #958 — LLM-as-judge quality scorer (m=3 majority voting) ~300 LoC
- [ ] #959 — Hindsight extraction from QA failures ~220 LoC
- [ ] #960 — Experiment loop skill for probe evolution ~250 LoC
- [ ] #961 — Case outcome aggregation for routing feedback ~180 LoC

### Phase 3: Architecture Changes

- [ ] #963 — Evolvable dispatch rules via experiment loop ~350 LoC
- [ ] #964 — Diversity-aware stepping-stone case archive ~350 LoC
- [ ] #966 — Pluggable AgentRunner trait in kernel TaskSupervisor ~250 LoC
- [ ] #967 — Quality-aware tool policy in kernel PolicyEngine ~180 LoC

### Skipped (with rationale)

- GPU-based RL training (Megatron/Ray/SGLang) — wrong infrastructure class for a Go+Rust CLI
- On-Policy Distillation — requires token-level logprobs and a second inference server
- Mixture-of-Agents ensemble — expensive (4+ LLM calls/query), non-deterministic, not suited for QA verification
- Multi-platform messaging gateway — Telegram integration is sufficient; consumer chat complexity irrelevant
- Browser automation tooling — loongclaw targets CLI agents, not web apps
- Plugin memory provider abstraction — 7 external backends add integration burden without QA benefit
items.author: gh-xj

items.title: fix: use require() for plugin API module loading
items.content:
## Problem
Plugin API proxy route uses `await import(plugin.apiRoutes)` with absolute filesystem paths (e.g. `/home/control/.openclaw/kitchen/plugins/node_modules/.../handler.js`). Next.js/webpack can't resolve these at build time, resulting in:
```
500 — Cannot find module as expression is too dynamic
```

## Fix
- Switch from `import()` to `require()` which resolves paths at runtime
- Clear `require.cache` on each request so plugin updates take effect without full Kitchen restart

## Tests
509/509 passing.
items.author: jiggai-automation-bot

items.title: fix: avoid AGFS collision in per-agent quick memory sidecar
items.content:
## Summary
- route per-agent quick-memory sidecar search through the existing OV HTTP service
- filter results by per-agent path scope instead of opening local embedded OV stores
- avoid AGFSManager bootstrap collisions with the launchd-managed AGFS service on 1833

## Why
The per-agent sidecar was using embedded/local `SyncOpenViking(path=store)` which triggers local AGFS startup. On this host AGFS is already managed by launchd (`com.openclaw.agfs-server`), so quick_memory_search could fail with `AGFS port 1833 is already in use`.

## Notes
- keeps `com.openclaw.openviking` on 8087 for now because this patch reuses it
- preserves practical per-agent scoping via `filter.path_contains` plus URI post-filtering in the sidecar
- follow-up cleanup can decide whether a cleaner true external-AGFS local client path is worth building later

## Validation
- direct OV HTTP filtering on 8087 returns agent-scoped results for `main`
- current 8091 sidecar/AGFS diagnosis confirms the AGFS collision is caused by embedded/local OV init, not plugin registration or launchd startup
items.author: xushen-ma

items.title: fix(win): re-enable native jiti loading for dist .js to prevent dual module instances
items.content:
## Summary

- Fixes #61614 — Feishu channel crash loop on Windows due to dual jiti/ESM module identity for the plugin runtime store
- Removes the blanket `win32` guard from `shouldPreferNativeJiti()` that was introduced in 3a4b96bf, which forced jiti to evaluate all dist `.js` chunks via `vm.runInThisContext` instead of Node's native ESM loader
- The actual Windows issue (backslash paths in jiti alias maps) is already handled by `normalizeJitiAliasTargetPath()`

## Root Cause

When `tryNative` is `false` on Windows, jiti creates its own module cache separate from Node's native ESM registry. Dynamic `import()` inside jiti-evaluated code escapes back to Node's ESM loader, creating a second instance of `runtime-BAUYLedo.js` with its own `createPluginRuntimeStore` closure. `setFeishuRuntime()` writes to jiti's closure during `register()`, but `getFeishuRuntime()` reads from the ESM closure when the monitor runs — which is still `null`.

## Testing

- `pnpm test src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts` — 27/27 pass
- `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts` — 134/134 pass
- `pnpm build` — clean
- Gateway startup verified: all 3 Feishu accounts start without "Feishu runtime not initialized" error
items.author: sanqiushu

items.title: Plugin contract test suite for SDK validation
items.content:
## Problem

There is no test harness that validates the invariants of LoongClaw's plugin contract from the outside — that is, from the perspective of a plugin author consuming the `plugin-sdk` crate. The `kernel` has unit tests for manifest parsing and activation, but nothing that systematically covers: (1) manifest schema conformance across all supported fields, (2) the relationship between declared capabilities and bridge kind, and (3) slot claim conflict detection. This means a breaking change to `PluginManifest` or `PluginBridgeKind` may silently accept malformed manifests until a runtime failure surfaces.

Openclaw ships ~80 contract test files in `openclaw/src/plugins/contracts/`. LoongClaw should establish an equivalent harness within the `plugin-sdk` crate's test module.

## What To Build

A `plugin-contract-test` module in `crates/plugin-sdk/src/` (or `crates/plugin-sdk/tests/`) covering three categories:

1. **Manifest schema conformance tests**:
   - Valid manifests for each supported bridge kind parse without error
   - Missing required fields (`id`, `version`, `bridge_kind`) produce clear `serde` deserialization errors
   - Unknown fields trigger warnings (or errors if `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` is set)
   - `host_api` version string round-trips through semver validation
   - `config_schema` field: valid JSON Schema object accepted, non-object value rejected

2. **Capability / bridge-kind consistency tests**:
   - A manifest declaring `bridge_kind: "http_json"` with capabilities that require local execution (e.g., filesystem access) should be flagged — these are contradictory (HTTP bridges cannot access the local filesystem)
   - A manifest declaring `bridge_kind: "native_ffi"` must have at least one capability declared (bare FFI plugin with no capabilities is a suspicious pattern)
   - Implement as a `validate_bridge_capability_consistency(manifest: &PluginManifest) -> Vec<ContractViolation>` function in `plugin-sdk`

3. **Slot claim conflict dete
items.author: gh-xj

items.title: OpenAI-compatible API surface in the gateway
items.content:
## Problem

LoongClaw's gateway control surface (`daemon/src/gateway/`) handles turn execution via internal protocol endpoints. There is no OpenAI-compatible HTTP surface, which means third-party tools — Continue, Cursor, LLM CLI, any OpenAI-compatible IDE plugin — cannot connect to loongclaw without custom integration. Openclaw's `openai-http.ts` demonstrates that exposing `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/models` endpoints on the existing HTTP server is the right approach: inbound OpenAI-formatted requests are transformed into gateway turn requests, and gateway turn responses are transformed back into OpenAI-formatted responses (both streaming SSE and non-streaming JSON).

The daemon already runs an axum HTTP server with turn handling, health endpoints, and bearer-token auth. The missing piece is the request/response transformation layer and the two compatibility route handlers.

This work depends on the transport trait (#972, for the transport-level request path) and the retry engine (#984, so the compatibility layer inherits retry behavior automatically).

## What To Build

Two new route handlers and a transformation layer added to `crates/daemon/src/gateway/`, following openclaw's `openai-http.ts` design.

**New routes**:
- `POST /v1/chat/completions` — accepts an OpenAI `ChatCompletionRequest`, returns either a `ChatCompletionResponse` (non-streaming) or an SSE stream of `ChatCompletionChunk` objects (streaming, when `stream: true`)
- `GET /v1/models` — returns an OpenAI `ModelListResponse` enumerating the providers and models configured in loongclaw's active config

**Request transformation** (`crates/daemon/src/gateway/openai_compat.rs`):
- Parse `ChatCompletionRequest`: `model`, `messages`, `stream`, `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `stop`
- Map `messages` (system/user/assistant roles) to loongclaw's internal turn message format
- Resolve `model` string to the matching `ProviderKind` + model identifier, or return 404 if not in the configured model list
- Build a

items.author: a-deal

items.title: Wire gateway to full agent loop with streaming support
items.content:
## Summary

- **Both gateway paths (HTTP + WebSocket) were bypassing the entire agent infrastructure** — calling `provider.chat(&messages, &[])` with empty tool arrays, making OpenClaw a plain chatbot with no tool-use capability
- Introduces an `orchestrate` module that wires the full pipeline: `HarnessRouter` profile selection → `SystemPromptBuilder` prompt construction → capability-scoped `Session` → `AgentRunner` execution with tool calls, memory compression, error reflection, and trace-informed guidance
- Adds `AgentEvent` enum and `AgentRunner::run_streaming()` so the WebSocket path streams real-time events (text deltas, tool start/end, reflection, compression) instead of blocking until the agent loop completes

### Changes by crate

| Crate | File | Change |
|-------|------|--------|
| `openclaw-core` | `capability.rs` | Add `CapabilitySet::for_tools()` — grants `FileRead`, `FileWrite`, `ShellExec`, `HttpRequest` + `Tool(name)` per tool |
| `openclaw-agent` | `runner.rs` | Add `AgentEvent` enum (6 variants) and `run_streaming()` method using `chat_stream` with event callback |
| `openclaw-agent` | `lib.rs` | Export `AgentEvent` |
| `openclaw-gateway` | `orchestrate.rs` | **New** — `run_agent()` (non-streaming) and `run_agent_streaming()` with shared `prepare_agent()` setup |
| `openclaw-gateway` | `state.rs` | Add `sandbox: Arc<dyn Sandbox>` field (defaults to `ProcessSandbox`) |
| `openclaw-gateway` | `routes.rs` | Rewire `send_message` to use `orchestrate::run_agent()` |
| `openclaw-gateway` | `ws.rs` | Rewire `handle_socket` to use `orchestrate::run_agent_streaming()` via mpsc channel |
| `openclaw-gateway` | `Cargo.toml` | Add `openclaw-skills` dependency |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` — clean compilation across all crates
- [x] `cargo test` — all existing tests pass
- [ ] Manual: start gateway, create conversation via `POST /api/conversations`, send message via `POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages`, verify tool-informed response
- [ ] Manual: conn
items.author: gcbh

items.title: [Bug]: No circuit breaker for model_cooldown — session retries indefinitely against hours-long cooldown
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

When all configured credentials return `model_cooldown` with a `reset_seconds` value indicating hours-long unavailability, OpenClaw has no circuit breaker. The session retries every ~20 seconds indefinitely, producing thousands of failed attempts and making the session completely unusable until manually reset.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Exhaust all claude-sonnet (or equivalent) credentials across all configured accounts so every credential returns `429 model_cooldown`
2. Send any inbound message to an affected agent session
3. Observe the session retry every ~20 seconds with no backoff and no halt condition
4. Session becomes an infinite retry loop — never surfaces an alert, never pauses, never auto-recovers



### Expected behavior

When all credentials return `model_cooldown` and `reset_seconds > 3600` (i.e. hours-long, not transient):

1. **Stop retrying immediately** — do not continue the ~20s retry loop
2. **Write a cooldown state marker** to the session (e.g. `cooling_until: <ISO timestamp>`) so the session knows it is in cooldown
3. **Surface an alert** to the user via the configured delivery channel (e.g. Telegram message: "All credentials cooling down until <time>. Will resume automatically.")
4. **Auto-resume** when `reset_seconds` has elapsed — either via a scheduled wake or on next gateway restart

For short cooldowns (`reset_seconds < 3600`) the current retry behaviour may be acceptable, but hours-long cooldowns should be treated as a distinct failure mode requiring a hard stop + user notification.


### Actual behavior

## Observed Behaviour (production incident, 2026-04-03/04)

A single inbound Telegram message was delivered to an agent session during a period when all `claude-sonnet-4-6` credentials were cooling down (reset time: ~102 hours). The session retried **every ~20 seconds for 16 hours** with zero circuit breaking:

- Total retry a

items.title: fix: Reduce output truncated — SSE response handling + stale Map cache
items.content:
Two issues found in production test (Mac Mini, 14 sources):

1. Reduce output truncated to ~500 chars (should be 1500-2500 words) Root cause: Proxy returns SSE format (data: {...}\n) but curl+jq only parsed standard JSON, getting just the first chunk. Fix: add stream:false to request body + SSE fallback parser that concatenates all delta.content chunks.

2. Map used stale cache (old 5-8 signal prompt, not new 10-15) Root cause: cache key was name_filesize, didn't include prompt version. Fix: cache key now includes md5 hash of MAP_PROMPT_TPL, so prompt changes automatically invalidate cache.

Also: fixed MODE_DESC UTF-8 truncation in log output.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DVZUZRMYu4LRCGqrgeHEJC

items.title: Docs: add explicit rollback instructions after applying the skill
items.content:
## Problem

The repo says to back up files first and to keep backups so rollback is easy, but it does not actually explain **how to roll back** after applying the skill.

From the current docs:
- `README.md` quick start says to back up `SOUL.md` and `USER.md`
- `README.md` line 46 says to "keep backups so rollback is easy"
- `skills/openclaw-terse/SKILL.md` says "Keep diffs small and rollback easy"

That is good advice, but a user still has to guess the actual restore procedure.

## Why this matters

This repo edits persona / behavior files. That makes rollback guidance more important than usual, especially for users testing tone changes and wanting a clean way back.

## Suggested fix

Add a short **Rollback** section to at least:
- `README.md`
- `README.zh.md`
- maybe `skills/openclaw-terse/SKILL.md`

Suggested contents:
- what files to restore (`SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, `RESPONSE_PROTOCOL.md` if changed)
- restore from the backup directory created before patching
- example commands or manual steps
- reminder to start a fresh session after restoring

## Nice-to-have

If the installer / workflow expects a conventional backup path or naming pattern, document that too.
items.author: BlueBirdBack

items.title: [Feature]: Thread-bound agent runtimes for Telegram topics (OpenClaw-like direct Codex/Claude/Gemini surfaces)
items.content:
Title: [Feature]: Thread-bound agent runtimes for Telegram topics (OpenClaw-like direct Codex/Claude/Gemini surfaces)

Problem or Use Case

I want Hermes gateway topics/threads to be bindable to a persistent external agent runtime so a Telegram topic can behave like a direct surface into Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or another coding agent.

Today Hermes supports:
- normal gateway conversations scoped by thread/topic
- ACP server mode for editors/IDEs
- limited external-process support for a single hard-coded provider path (`copilot-acp`)
- `delegate_task` ACP transport override for child agents

But it does not support the OpenClaw-style workflow where the current messaging thread becomes a bound session for an external agent runtime.

Concrete use cases:
- A Telegram topic named “Gemini” that routes every message into a persistent Gemini CLI session
- A Telegram topic named “Claude Code” that routes every message into a persistent Claude Code session
- A Telegram topic named “Codex” that routes every message into a persistent Codex session
- Switching a thread back to normal Hermes chat via an explicit unbind command
- Persistent follow-up in the same thread without re-spawning or re-explaining context each turn

The desired UX is not just “topic-specific prompt/personality/profile”. It is “this topic is now directly attached to an external agent runtime/session”.

Proposed Solution

Add a generic bound-runtime / thread-binding system for gateway sessions, starting with Telegram topics.

High-level behavior:
1. User binds the current topic/thread to an agent runtime (`/agent gemini`, `/agent claude`, `/agent codex`, or equivalent)
2. Hermes creates or resumes a persistent runtime session for that agent
3. Hermes stores a binding from the current conversation/thread to that runtime session
4. Future inbound messages in that topic bypass ordinary Hermes chat routing and are forwarded to the bound runtime
5. Hermes streams runtime output back into the same topic
6

items.title: Implement Fleet Inference Optimization: Lobster Workflow with ali-adk & GitHub Integration
items.content:
# Fleet Inference Optimization — Team Workflow

## Context

A team-style software delivery workflow: Lobster provides the deterministic step sequencer
(outer shell); ali-agentic-adk-python provides the iterative reasoning agent inside each
step; LangGraph augments where stateful checkpointing and conditional branching are needed.
DSPy optimizes per-step prompts. AutoAgent optimizes workflow topology post-run (N=10 cadence).

GitHub integration is woven into the Lobster workflow — issue created at plan, comments
posted on each step/iteration, PR opened on successful completion. `gh` CLI is already in
fleet base packages (`modules/base.nix`), so no new tooling needed.

**On ali-langengine**: ali-langengine-core is Java-only (Maven, no Python bridge). Cannot
be used directly. `ali-agentic-adk-python` wraps Google ADK (Python) and provides the same
ReAct/Plan-Execute patterns Qwen3.5 was trained on. Alignment holds through pattern ancestry.

`ali-agentic-adk-python` depends on `google-adk` (already in fleet at `pkgs/google-adk/`).

---

## The Workflow

```
Wanda (OpenClaw gateway)
  └── Lobster: team-workflow.yaml  [deterministic step sequencer]
        │
        ├── team-plan   ─→ ali-adk Plan agent (ReAct)
        │                   Decompose task, identify risks, structure approach
        │               ─→ gh issue create  (plan.md as body, issue# stored)
        │
        ├── team-prd    ─→ ali-adk Plan agent (ReAct)
        │                   Produce spec: acceptance criteria, constraints, interfaces
        │               ─→ gh issue comment  (PRD output posted to issue)
        │
        ├── team-exec   ─→ ali-adk Execute agent + LangGraph (stateful, conditional)
        │                   Implement against PRD; each iteration posts a comment
        │               ─→ gh issue comment  (exec status: files changed, test results)
        │
        ├── team-verify ─→ ali-adk Execute agent + LangGraph (conditional branching)
        │                   Run te

items.title: feat(settings): add optional hidden workspace entries toggle
items.content:
## What

Adds an optional hidden-workspace-entries toggle for the Nerve workspace browser.

- introduces a user-facing setting for showing hidden workspace files/folders
- defaults it to **off** so existing users see no UI change
- includes hidden entries in workspace tree loading when enabled
- keeps normal open/reveal flows working for hidden entries while enabled

Closes #245.

## Why

Nerve's default hidden-entry behavior is reasonable for most users, but some developer workflows need access to dotfiles, hidden documentation folders, repo metadata, and other hidden workspace artifacts.

This slice adds that capability in a generalized, opt-in way instead of baking special handling for any one hidden path. That preserves the uncluttered default UI while giving advanced users a way to opt into deeper workspace browsing.

## How

- added `showHiddenWorkspaceEntries` state to settings with localStorage persistence
- exposed the setting in Appearance settings as **Show hidden workspace entries**
- threaded the setting through workspace tree loading
- updated server-side file-tree listing to respect the hidden-entry flag
- ensured reveal/open flows stay coherent for hidden entries when the setting is enabled
- added focused tests for tree loading, panel behavior, and server-side hidden-entry handling

## Validation

- `npx vitest run server/routes/file-browser.test.ts src/features/file-browser/hooks/useFileTree.test.ts src/features/file-browser/FileTreePanel.test.tsx`
- `npm run build`
- local dogfood:
  - hidden entries stay hidden by default
  - enabling the toggle shows hidden entries in the workspace tree
  - disabling it hides them again
  - persisted behavior works across restart/reopen

## Notes / out of scope

- This PR does not redesign broader permissions or search/index behavior for hidden entries.

## Type of change

- ✨ New feature

items.title: Browser start reports CDP unreachable even while managed Chromium is live and /json/version responds
items.content:
### Summary
On this OpenClaw 2026.4.2 Linux host, the official managed browser lane for profile `openclaw` is degraded even though Chromium/CDP itself is healthy.

### Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.2
- Host: Linux VPS
- Browser config (effective baseline during investigation):
  - `browser.enabled=true`
  - `browser.defaultProfile=openclaw`
  - `browser.executablePath=/home/openclaw/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1208/chrome-linux64/chrome`
  - `browser.headless=true`
  - `browser.noSandbox=true`
  - `browser.profiles.openclaw.cdpPort=18800`
- Browser plugin enabled

### What happens
`openclaw browser start` reports a browser start for profile `openclaw`, but the CLI then fails with:
- `Chrome CDP websocket for profile "openclaw" is not reachable after start`

Related observed states across attempts:
- `openclaw browser status` => `running: false`, `browser: unknown`, `detectedBrowser: custom` (or `unknown` when testing auto-detect), `cdpPort: 18800`
- `openclaw browser tabs` => either websocket-unreachable or `No tabs (browser closed or no targets)`
- `openclaw browser open` / `snapshot` can fail with `PortInUseError: Port 18800 is already in use`

### Critical evidence
The important part is that Chromium/CDP is actually healthy during the failing window:
- OpenClaw logs lines like `openclaw browser started (custom) profile "openclaw" on 127.0.0.1:18800 (pid ...)`
- Parallel inspection during a failing `openclaw browser start` showed:
  - a live Chromium process tree still running after the failure window
  - a real listener on `127.0.0.1:18800`
  - `http://127.0.0.1:18800/json/version` returns successfully
  - a valid `webSocketDebuggerUrl` is present

So the failure is not simply “browser did not launch” or “CDP is absent”. It looks more like OpenClaw’s managed-profile attach/validation/state logic falsely concludes the profile is unreachable.

### Additional narrowing already done
The following were tested and did not resolve it:
- resetting/clearing the managed

items.content:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: OpenClaw still shipped a bundled OpenTelemetry exporter plus telemetry-specific config and SDK surfaces.
- Why it matters: The requested behavior is to remove telemetry code paths without breaking core gateway behavior.
- What changed: Deleted the `diagnostics-otel` bundled extension, removed telemetry-only config/docs/package wiring, turned diagnostic event subscriptions into inert compatibility shims, and stripped telemetry emissions from runtime code.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Local diagnostics logging, session-state tracking, and cache-trace debugging remain available.

## OpenClaw Deep Dive

**OpenClaw Project Digest – April 6, 2026**

**1. Today's Overview**  
OpenClaw remains highly active with 500 issues and 500 PRs updated in the last 24 hours, reflecting sustained community engagement and development momentum. The project shows no new releases today, but a high volume of bug fixes, regression patches, and feature enhancements across agents, channels, memory, and authentication subsystems. Overall activity indicates a mature, responsive codebase under active maintenance with strong user-driven iteration.

**2. Releases**  
No new releases were published as of 2026-04-06. The latest stable version remains 2026.3.28 (from March 28), though multiple beta-level regressions have been addressed since then.

**3. Project Progress**  
Several significant PRs merged or closed today include:  
- **PR #61547**: Added Amazon Bedrock as a native embedding provider for memory search, enabling Titan and Cohere models via AWS SDK.  
- **PR #61568**: Unified plugin update/install argument handling across npm, ClawHub, and marketplace flows, improving CLI consistency.  
- **PR #61555 / #61533 / #61523**: Completed in-process Lobster integration, enabling managed TaskFlow workflows within OpenClaw runtime.

以下是 2026-04-06 主流 AI CLI 工具生态的横向对比分析报告：

---

# 2026-04-06 AI CLI 工具生态横向对比分析报告

## 1. 🏔️ 生态全景
当前 AI CLI 工具生态正从单纯的**代码生成器**向**自主工程化 Agent** 演进。**Claude Code** 与 **OpenAI Codex** 虽占据头部流量，但正陷入“大厂病”困境，近期版本频繁出现严重的性能回退与计费异常，用户信任度承压。与此同时，**Charm Crush**、**Qwen Code** 等中间层工具凭借极快的迭代速度和对特定场景（如 TUI 体验、国内环境）的深度适配，正在通过修复头部产品的痛点（如 Windows 兼容性、Agent 配置自省）来争夺开发者心智。**跨平台兼容性**（尤其是 Windows）和**上下文管理**（Token 计费与长对话记忆）已成为全行业共同面临的“天花板”难题。

---

## 2. 📊 各工具活跃度对比

| 工具名称 | Issue 活跃度 (24h) | PR 活跃度 (24h) | 版本发布状态 | 核心状态关键词 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Claude Code** | 🔥 极高 (聚焦计费/跨平台) | 中 (安全修复/开源尝试) | 无新版本 | **🚨 信任危机** (计费异常/数据丢失) |
| **OpenAI Codex** | 🔥 极高 (聚焦性能/CPU) | 高 (架构重构/音频栈) | 无新版本 | **⚠️ 稳定性崩坏** (内核恐慌/资源占用) |
| **Gemini CLI** | 🟢 中 (架构讨论/Shell注入) | 极高 (核心重构/体验) | 无新版本 | **🚀 快速进化** (上下文管理/LSP) |
| **GitHub Copilot CLI** | 🟡 中低 (Win兼容/配置) | 低 (PR关闭为主) | 无新版本 | **🐌 维护缓慢** (Win无报错崩溃) |
| **Charm Crush** | 🟡 低 (LSP/Docker Bug) | 中 (自省能力) | nightly | **🛠️ 细节打磨** (Agent工具链) |
| **Qwen Code** | 🔥 高 (UI渲染/登录) | 极高 (Agent自动化) | 无新版本 | **🔥 社区驱动** (高频修复/体验) |

| **OpenCode** | 🔥 高 (计费/内存/集成) | 高 (TUI升级/修复) | 无新版本 | **📈 功能膨胀** (Agent Teams/内存) |

items.title: Feature Request: skill:install Hook for Pre-Install Security Vetting
items.content:
## Problem

Currently, OpenClaw does not support a Hook event that fires before a Skill is installed. The Hook system provides events like `command:new`, `session:*`, `agent:*`, `message:*`, and `before_tool_call` (plugin), but there is no way to intercept and vet a Skill before it gets installed.

## Use Case

Users want to automatically run security scans (skill-vetter) before any Skill is installed, to prevent malicious or poorly-written Skills from executing dangerous commands like `rm -rf /`, `dd`, fork bombs, curl|sh, etc.

## Proposed Solution

Add a new Hook event: `skill:install:before` that fires when:
1. A skill installation is requested via ClawHub (clawhub install)
2. A skill is installed via npm (npm i -g <skill>)
3. Any programmatic skill installation

The Hook should receive:
```typescript
{
  type: "skill",
  action: "install:before",
  skillName: string,       // e.g., "ontology"
  skillPath: string,       // local path or registry slug
  installMethod: string,   // "clawhub" | "npm" | "manual"
  sessionKey: string,
  timestamp: Date,
  messages: string[],     // push warning/block message here
}
```

The Hook can:
- **Block**: Set `block: true` to prevent installation
- **Warn**: Add warning message to `messages[]` to alert user
- **Mutate**: Modify `skillPath` to point to a local copy for vetting first

## Workaround (Current)

Currently using a `message:received` hook to detect install commands and manually invoke skill-vetter, but this cannot actually block the installation - only warns after the fact.

## References

- OpenClaw Hooks documentation: docs/automation/hooks.md
- Existing skill-vetter implementation: ~/.openclaw/skills/skill-vetter
- Related: Claude Code's PreToolUse Hook pattern

---

Feature Req: **skill:install Hook** (priority: high for enterprise/team deployments)

items.title: feat(cli): goals status overview, workspace skill auto-deploy, injectionBudget schema
items.content:
## Summary

- **`goals status`** — Running without arguments now prints an **overview** (goal stewardship on/off, goals directory, active goals table). `goals status <label-or-uuid>` still shows full detail; `--json` works for both modes.
- **Workspace skill** — On plugin startup, the bundled `skills/hybrid-memory/` tree is copied into `{workspace}/skills/hybrid-memory/` **only when `SKILL.md` is missing**, so operators do not rely solely on `hybrid-mem install` for first-time deploy. Existing files are not overwritten each restart; use **`hybrid-mem install`** to refresh from the bundle.
- **`activeTask.injectionBudget`** — `openclaw.plugin.json` uses **`integer`** with **`minimum: 1`** to match parsing (fractional values floored; invalid/non-positive falls back to default 500). Types and parser comments updated.

## Docs

- `docs/CLI-REFERENCE.md`, `docs/GOAL-STEWARDSHIP-OPERATOR.md`, bundled `SKILL.md`, README, workspace TOOLS snippet, `CHANGELOG.md` ([Unreleased]).

## Tests

- `install-workspace-skill.test.ts` covers `ensureHybridMemoryWorkspaceSkillIfMissing`.


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

items.title: Proposal: Behavioral session profiling for targeted CLI wrapper abuse detection
items.content:
## Summary

I built a tool that distinguishes legitimate Claude Code developers from CLI wrapper abuse by analyzing session behavioral data that Claude Code already collects. It's open source, zero dependencies (Python 3.8+ stdlib), and runs entirely locally.

**Repo:** https://github.com/Sn3th/claude-k2-session-guard

## Why

CLI wrapper tools (OpenClaw, claude-code-provider, horselock, etc.) abuse the Claude Code CLI as a free API proxy. This is a real problem costing Anthropic real compute. But the current mitigation degrades sessions for all users equally, including developers using the product exactly as intended: dev on CLI, production on the API.

The behavioral difference between a real developer and a CLI wrapper is massive. This tool measures it and proves targeted detection is viable.

## What It Measures

**Session-level signals (per session):**
- Idle ratio (humans think and pause, wrappers don't)
- Cadence variance (humans have irregular timing, wrappers are uniform)
- Prompt entropy (humans use diverse vocabulary, wrappers are templated)
- Corrections ("no", "wait", "actually" = human. Zero corrections = bot)
- Tool diversity (developers use Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, etc. Wrappers use 1-2 tools or zero)
- Scaffold detection (machine-generated `<tool_result>` XML, serialized JSON schemas)
- Single-turn sessions (one prompt in, one response out = subprocess shim signature)

**Account-level signals (across all sessions + git):**
- Developer vs automated session ratio and proportionality
- Git commit correlation (legitimate automation is proportional to commits)
- Commit cadence, daily variance, peak hours, co-authors
- Sessions-per-commit ratio

## Results Against Real Data

Tested against 6,800+ sessions and 1,800+ commits from a single developer account:

```
Sessions (6,830 total)
  Developer-side:   1,805 (26%)
  Automated:        2,459 (36%)
  Ratio:            1.4:1 auto:dev

Git: 1,824 commits across 4 repos in 64 days
  28.5 commits/day
  3.7 sessi

items.title: v0.1.8: add build step, ship dist/index.js, fix OpenClaw 2026.4.x compatibility
items.content:
## Problem
OpenClaw 2026.4.x requires plugins to ship compiled JavaScript. The previous version shipped raw TypeScript (`src/index.ts`) which caused:
- Gateway to fail loading the plugin entirely
- Config entries silently rejected as 'plugin not found'

## Fix
- Add `tsc` build script + `prepublishOnly` hook so `dist/index.js` is always present on publish
- Update `tsconfig.json` to emit JS to `dist/`
- Point `openclaw.extensions` to `./dist/index.js`
- Add `dist/` to `package.files`

## Notes
- `WAGL_DB` env var already supported as fallback for `dbPath` config (needed for 2026.4.x strict schema validation)
- Tested on OpenClaw 2026.4.2 — plugin registers successfully

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Updated build configuration to compile TypeScript and include the compiled output in the published package.

items.author: xiaojiou176

items.title: [Feature]: Expose public mapping from hashed agent scope to logical agent_id
items.content:
### Problem Statement

OpenViking stores agent-scoped memories under hashed URIs such as viking://agent/8h56gb39sbgb/..., while client integrations often know the logical agent id they sent, such as default, openclaw, or codex. At the moment, I could not find a public API that lets clients reverse-resolve the hashed agent scope back to the logical agent_id. This makes it difficult to build admin dashboards and multi-agent UIs, because integrations can only display opaque hashed agent ids instead of meaningful agent names.

### Proposed Solution

Expose a supported public way to resolve hashed agent scopes to their logical agent_id. This could be done by adding an endpoint such as GET /api/v1/agents or GET /api/v1/agents/{scope}, or by including logical agent metadata in existing responses for agent URIs such as ls, stat, or memory metadata responses. A response that includes both the hashed scope and logical agent id, for example owner_space: "8h56gb39sbgb" and agent_id: "codex", would make multi-agent workspace integrations much easier to build.

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Feature Area

Core (Client/Engine)

### Use Case

A single workspace may have multiple runtimes writing agent memories, for example OpenClaw, Codex, internal automations, or support bots. In a admin UI, we want to show which logical agent created cases and patterns, not just the hashed viking://agent/<hash>/... scope. A public reverse-mapping would allow integrations to present clear agent attribution and keep multi-agent memory inspection understandable for users and admins.

### Example API (Optional)

```python

```

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Contribution

- [ ] I am willing to contribute to implementing this feature

items.title: docs(services): add ADR for Discord chat automation & reactivity
items.content:
## Summary

- Evaluates NanoClaw and OpenClaw patterns (scheduling, triggers, proactive posting, per-channel memory) and identifies what to steal vs. skip
- Proposes building on the existing PydanticAI + pgvector + PostgreSQL stack rather than adopting either framework wholesale
- Formalizes the existing `user_channel_summaries` system: adds channel-level summaries, auto-injects summaries into agent context, improves prompts with rolling-window awareness
- Adds three new tables: `scheduled_tasks` (cron/one-shot polling), `triggers` (regex pattern matching), `channel_memory` (persistent per-channel notes)
- Three new PydanticAI tools so users can create automations conversationally

## Phased rollout

1. **Formalize summaries** — channel-level summaries, auto-injection into context, configurable prompts (low risk, extends what works)
2. **Scheduled tasks** — reminders, digests, migrate hardcoded 24h summary loop into DB
3. **Event triggers** — regex pattern matching on messages with cooldowns
4. **Channel memory & proactive posting** — persistent notes, bot posts without being mentioned

## Test plan

- [ ] Review ADR for completeness and accuracy
- [ ] Validate schema designs against existing `chat` schema
- [ ] Discuss open questions (permissions, timeouts, summary token budget, cron library)

items.title: 构建跨平台 Agent 工具映射层
items.content:
## 背景

obra/superpowers v5.0.7 提供了 Claude Code ↔ Copilot CLI 工具映射表，包括 Skill tool、Task tool、异步 Bash sessions 等工具的完整对照。这启发我们：如果能构建一个统一的抽象层，让同一个 skill 可以同时在 Claude Code、Copilot CLI、OpenClaw 等平台上运行，将大幅提升 skill 的可移植性。

## 目标

创建一个 adapter 层，让 skill 开发者编写一次，可以在多个 AI Coding 平台上运行。

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析各平台工具语义差异（Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Task 等）
- [ ] 设计统一的工具抽象接口
- [ ] 实现 OpenClaw ↔ Claude Code ↔ Copilot CLI 的 adapter
- [ ] 编写测试验证跨平台兼容性
- [ ] 文档化适配器使用方式
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: 【提效想法#2】OpenClaw Loop Operator Agent
items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 实现了一个 `loop-operator` agent，专门负责运行自主 agent 循环：

```yaml
name: loop-operator
description: Operate autonomous agent loops, monitor progress, and intervene safely when loops stall.
```

**核心能力：**
- 启动循环时验证 quality gates、eval baseline、rollback path
- 跟踪 progress checkpoints
- 检测 stall 和 retry storm
- 失败时自动 pause + scope reduction
- escalation 机制（成本漂移、merge 冲突等）

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 实现一个 Loop Operator skill/agent，用于：
1. 编排复杂的多步骤 AI Coding 任务
2. 防止无意义的重复循环
3. 自动降级和恢复

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 loop-operator agent 源码（agents/loop-operator.md）
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw 版本的质量门禁检查清单
- [ ] 实现 checkpoint 跟踪机制
- [ ] 添加 stall/retry 检测逻辑
- [ ] 实现自动降级策略（scope reduction）
- [ ] 与 OpenClaw cron 系统集成，实现定时检查

## 启发来源

- `agents/loop-operator.md`
- `skills/autonomous-loops/`

items.title: Research: verify pi harness on fourty4 — import path + ACP SDK API shape
items.content:
## What this is

Pre-build verification for koad/vulcan#39 (pi harness integration). Vulcan is ready to build Phase 1 but the plan gates it on two verifications that can only happen on fourty4. These are research tasks for Sibyl.

## Task 1: Verify `createAgentSession` import via jiti

SSH to fourty4 and run:

```bash
JITI="/Users/koad/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/jiti/bin/jiti.js"
node "$JITI" -e "
import { createAgentSession } from '/Users/koad/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/src/index.ts';
console.log('OK:', typeof createAgentSession);
"
```

**Report:**
- Does it work? (exits 0, prints "OK: function")
- If not: what is the error? (jiti version conflict? wrong path? missing file?)
- If conflict: does `npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` resolve it? What path does it install to?

## Task 2: Read ACP SDK API shape

On fourty4, inspect the installed `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` package:

```bash
cat /Users/koad/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/@agentclientprotocol/sdk/dist/index.js | head -200
# or
cat /Users/koad/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/@agentclientprotocol/sdk/README.md
```

**Report:**
- What is the exported API? Specifically: is there an `AgentServer` class? What are its constructor options and methods?
- Does `onTask(task, stream)` exist? What is the shape of `task` and `stream`?
- Is `stream.sendEvent()` real? What does it accept?
- Is there an `AgentClient` class? What does `client.run()` return?
- What version of the SDK is installed?

## Why this matters

- Task 1 is a go/no-go gate for Phase 1. If jiti can't import pi, Vulcan needs the fallback path before writing any code.
- Task 2 is needed before Phase 4 (ACP server). Medium-confidence from prior research — Vulcan needs the actual API shape to write correct code.

## Deliverable

A short research note at `~/.sibyl/rese

items.title: fix(recurrence): extract shouldShowOnDate, fix habit filtering in day+week view, remove weekly frequency
items.content:
## What this fixes

Closes part of #90.

### Bugs fixed
- **Week view** showed ALL habits every day — `getItemsForDay` was doing `const dayHabits = habits` with zero filtering
- **Day view** had a sloppy `weekly` fallback in the inline switch that returned `true` (show every day) when `repeatDays` was unset
- **Monthly** fallback also returned `true` when `repeatMonthDay` was unset (now returns `false`)

### Changes
- New `lib/recurrence.ts` — `shouldShowOnDate(item, dateStr, userTimezone)` + `toDateStr(date, tz)` shared utilities. Both day view and week view now use these instead of duplicated inline switch statements.
- Removed `weekly` from `RepeatFrequencySchema` — was already hidden from all form dropdowns, replaced by `custom`. Migration 014 converts any existing `weekly` rows to `custom` and adds DB-level check constraints.
- Form validation: `custom` frequency now blocks submit with 0 days selected (UI disabled button + helper text + Zod-level `.superRefine()`)
- Unit tests updated to import from `lib/recurrence` instead of duplicating the helper inline

## Testing
- 13 unit tests pass
- `pnpm build` clean
- Run migration 014 in Supabase before deploying

## What's NOT in this PR
Task recurrence propagation (recurring tasks showing on future matching days) — that's PR B, coming next.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Repeat-day selector now appears only for "custom" repeats, shows inline "Select at least one day" validation, and disables Add/Save when no days are chosen.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved timezone-aware recurrence logic so habits and tasks display correctly for users in different time zones.

* **Chores**
  * Removed the "Weekly" repeat option; existing weekly entries are migrated to "custom" and DB constraints enforce allowed values.

* **Tests**
  * Recurrence tests updated to use centralized helpers and expanded to cover daily behavior.

items.author: kjswalls

items.title: feat(workspace): add navigable markdown document view
items.content:
## What

Adds a navigable markdown document view for workspace-opened markdown files.

- workspace-opened `.md` / `.markdown` files now open in a markdown document surface
- the document view supports a default **Preview** mode plus **Edit** fallback
- external URLs remain clickable
- workspace file/directory links use the existing resolver-backed open/reveal flow
- relative markdown links can resolve from the current document safely within the workspace
- same-document anchors stay in-app instead of opening a new browser tab/page

Closes #243.

## Why

Nerve already has useful markdown/link behavior in chat-rendered contexts, but markdown files opened from the workspace were previously treated only as editable text. That made docs, READMEs, notes, and linked markdown workflows less useful than they could be.

This slice adds a generalized markdown document-navigation capability rather than a workflow-specific surface. It improves markdown usability for a broad set of Nerve users without introducing a specialized plans/docs system.

## How

- added a dedicated `MarkdownDocumentView` for workspace-opened markdown files
- reused existing `MarkdownRenderer` and app-level workspace open/reveal plumbing
- extended `/api/files/resolve` with safe `relativeTo` support for current-document-relative links
- added heading ids and in-document anchor handling for `#section` links
- preserved workspace safety checks so escape traversal still fails safely
- kept editing available through an explicit **Edit** mode

## Validation

- `npm test -- --run src/features/markdown/MarkdownRenderer.test.tsx server/routes/file-browser.test.ts`
- `npx vitest run src/features/markdown/MarkdownRenderer.test.tsx server/routes/file-browser.test.ts`
- `npx tsc -b`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run lint`
- local dogfood:
  - markdown files open in Preview mode by default
  - same-document anchors work in-app
  - regular workspace links and external URLs behave correctly
  - blocked traversal still fails
items.author: DerrickBarra

items.title: New Source
items.content:
Source URL: Continual learning for AI agents
HARRISON'S IN THE LOOP SERIES 4 MIN READ APR 5, 2026
Most discussions of continual learning in AI focus on one thing: updating model
weights. But for AI agents, learning can happen at three distinct layers: the model,
the harness, and the context. Understanding the di!erence changes how you think
about building systems that improve over time.
The three main layers of agentic systems are:
Model: the model weights themselves.
Harness: the harness around the model that powers all instances of the agent.
This refers to the code that drives the agent, as well as any instructions or tools
that are always part of the harness.
Context: additional context (instructions, skills) that lives outside the harness,
and can be used to configure it.
Example #1: Mapping this a coding agent like Claude Code:
Model: claude-sonnet, etc
Harness: Claude Code
User context: CLAUDE.md, /skills, mcp.json
Example #2: Mapping this to OpenClaw:
Model: many
Harness: Pi + some other sca!olding
Agent context: SOUL.md, skills from clawhub
When we talk about continual learning, most people jump immediately to the
model. But in reality - an AI system can learn at all three of these levels.
SFT, RL (e.g. GRPO), etc.
Continual learning at the model layer
When most people talk about continual learning, this is what they most commonly
refer to: updating the model weights.
Techniques to update this include A central challenge here is catastrophic forgetting — when a model is updated on
new data or tasks, it tends to degrade on things it previously knew. This is an open
research problem.
When people do train models for a specific agentic system (e.g. you could view the
OpenAI codex models as being trained for their Codex agent) they largely do this for
the agentic system as a whole. In theory, you could do this at a more granular level
(e.g. you could have a LORA per user) but in practice this is mostly done at the
agent level.
Continual learning at the harness l

## 横向对比

# AI CLI 工具生态横向对比分析报告 | 2026-04-06

---

## 1. 生态全景

当前 AI CLI 工具生态呈现**"头部三极+新兴追赶"**格局：Claude Code 凭借成熟度和付费用户基础占据企业心智，但计费透明度危机正在侵蚀信任；OpenAI Codex 以激进的技术迭代（WebRTC 实时音视频、深度分析埋点）试图差异化突围，却同样陷入 Token 消耗黑洞的争议；Google Gemini CLI 依托内部工程资源推进架构级创新（AST 感知、内存路由、LSP 集成），但稳定性问题制约口碑。二线工具（Kimi、OpenCode、Pi、Qwen）则通过快速功能对标、开源策略或垂直场景深耕争夺剩余市场，技术栈重构（Python→TypeScript）成为普遍选择。

---

## 2. 各工具活跃度对比

| 工具 | 今日 Issues | 今日 PRs | 版本发布 | 核心动态 |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **Claude Code** | 50+ | 5 | ❌ 无 | 计费危机 #38335（427评论）主导讨论，开源呼声高涨 |
| **OpenAI Codex** | 50+ | 15+ | ❌ 无 | 实时音视频 4-PR 技术栈、分析埋点 6-PR 集群推进 |
| **Gemini CLI** | 50 | 50 | ❌ 无 | 工具输出优化、AST 感知、内存路由等架构级功能密集迭代 |
| **GitHub Copilot CLI** | 14 | 3（全关闭） | ❌ 无 | Windows 稳定性危机 #1164 持续 2 个月未解 |
| **Kimi Code CLI** | 8（新爆发） | 6 | ❌ 无 | v1.30.0 稳定性危机，Python→TS 重构提案引发架构讨论 |
| **OpenCode** | 50+ | 10+ | v1.3.15 | 计费异常 #8030（210+评论）+ 版本回归问题双重危机 |
| **Pi** | 25 | 13 | **v0.65.2** | 23/25 Issue 当日关闭，92% 解决率，新模型适配活跃 |
| **Qwen Code** | 34 | 10+ | ❌ 无 | wenshao 单日 7 PR，终端体验与权限策略成焦点 |

> **活跃度排序**：Gemini CLI ≈ Claude Code ≈ OpenAI Codex > OpenCode > Qwen Code > Pi > Kimi > Copilot CLI

---

## 3. 共同关注的功能方向

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

**OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-04-06）**

---

### 1. **今日速览**

过去24小时，OpenClaw 社区活跃度极高，Issues 和 PRs 各更新 500 条，反映用户参与度与贡献强度均处于高位。整体项目状态稳定，无新版本发布，但修复与功能推进并行。核心议题集中在身份验证、代理集成、模型路由回归及多语言支持请求上，技术债清理持续推进。

---

### 2. **版本发布**

无新版本发布。

---

### 3. **项目进展**

本周期内合并的重要 PR 包括：
- **[PR #61555] Lobster: add managed TaskFlow mode**  
  实现插件端可创建和恢复托管 TaskFlow，提升工作流自动化能力，为后续 Greptile 集成打下基础。  
  [openclaw/openclaw PR #61555](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61555)

- **[PR #61568] refactor: share plugin update install args**  
  统一 npm、ClawHub 和 marketplace 的插件更新参数处理逻辑，减少代码重复并增强安全性。  
  [openclaw/openclaw PR #61568](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61568)

- **[PR #61291] fix: clear stale model-selection overrides in cron session resolution**  
  修复定时任务因继承聊天会话过期的模型覆盖配置而重试失败的问题，提升 cron 任务稳定性。  
  [openclaw/openclaw PR #61291](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61291)

此外，Bedrock 嵌入支持（#61547）、iMessageLite 标准化（#61561）等基础设施改进也已完成或接近合并，表明平台在云原生与跨平台适配方面持续演进。

---

### 4. **社区热点**

- **[Issue #49971] RFC: Native Agent Identity & Trust Verification for OpenClaw**  
  来自 CryptoKRI GmbH 的提案，呼吁建立去中心化代理身份与信任验证机制，引用 ERC-8004 与 W3C DID/VC 标准。虽目前无直接实现计划，但引发对安全架构升级的关注。  
  [openclaw/openclaw Issue #49971](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/issue

OpenClaw 项目今日维持极高活跃度，过去24小时内共处理了 **500 条 Issues** 和 **500 条 Pull Requests**，社区讨论热度不减。尽管没有发布新的版本标签，但代码库正在经历密集的功能迭代与架构重构，特别是在 **记忆搜索扩展**（Bedrock Embedding Provider）、**国际化支持** 以及 **Lobster 工作流引擎** 的内联集成方面有重大进展。同时，多项涉及最新版本（2026.4.x）的 **回归 Bug** 被紧急报告，主要集中在模型注册、会话管理和超时处理上，提示近期版本的稳定性需引起关注。

## 🚀 版本发布
*无新版本发布。*
*(注：尽管没有发布新的 Release，但从 Issues 日期推断，项目似乎已推进至 2026.4.x 开发版本)*

## 🛠️ 项目进展
今日合并或活跃的核心 PR 主要集中在底层架构优化与新功能接入：

*   **架构重构与记忆增强**: 
    *   **[PR #61547](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61547)**: 引入 Amazon Bedrock 作为原生记忆搜索的嵌入提供商，支持 Titan 和 Cohere 模型，通过 AWS SDK 集成。
    *   **[PR #61568](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61568)**: 重构了插件更新安装参数的共享逻辑，统一了 npm、ClawHub 和 Marketplace 的更新流。
*   **国际化 (i18n) 推进**:
    *   **[PR #61553](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61553)**: 修复了 Web UI 中 Agent 标签页的硬编码字符串问题，将其替换为翻译键，覆盖了包括中、英、日、法在内的 12 种语言。
*   **Runtime 与稳定性修复**:
    *   **[PR #61566](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61566)**: 加固了 Lobster 扩展与嵌入式 Runtime 的集成。
    *   **[PR #61565](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61565)**: 修复了 Gateway WebSocket 关闭时的挂起问题，确保重启流程顺畅。

items.title: 🔍 系统性调试 Framework（Iron Law）
items.content:
## 背景

gstack 新增了 gstack-openclaw-investigate skill，核心理念是"Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST"。4 阶段调试：investigate → analyze → hypothesize → implement。调试验证前必须先找到 root cause，否则只是修症状。

## 目标

在 ai-coding-fullstack 中实现一个 AI Coding 调试 agent，遵循 root cause first 原则，不盲目修 bug。

## 子任务

- [ ] 1. 实现 4 阶段调试流程（investigate/analyze/hypothesize/implement）
- [ ] 2. 实现 git log 历史追溯（自动查找相关变更）
- [ ] 3. 实现 pattern matching（race condition, nil/null propagation, state corruption 等）
- [ ] 4. 实现 memory search（检查是否有历史调试记录）
- [ ] 5. 实现"先报 hypothesis 再修"的用户确认流程
- [ ] 6. 集成到 AI Coding 工作流中

## 标签

enhancement, debugging, agent

items.author: foryourhealth111-pixel

items.title: fix(config): reject heartbeat.every values that overflow Node.js setTimeout
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: Setting `heartbeat.every` to values > ~596h (e.g., `99999h`) causes Node.js `setTimeout` to silently overflow. The 32-bit signed integer limit (~2,147,483,647ms) clamps the delay to 1ms, firing heartbeats every millisecond and breaking channel message processing.
- Why it matters: Users who set large values to effectively disable heartbeats get the opposite behavior — rapid-fire heartbeats that exhaust resources and break message delivery.
- What changed: Added overflow validation in `HeartbeatSchema.superRefine()` to reject values exceeding the setTimeout limit at config load time with a clear error message.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): `parseDurationMs()`, `heartbeat-runner.ts`, `heartbeat-summary.ts` — only the Zod schema validation layer is touched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61546
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `HeartbeatSchema.superRefine()` validated that the duration string parses correctly but did not check whether the parsed millisecond value exceeds Node.js's 32-bit setTimeout limit.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No range validation on parsed duration values in the config schema.
- Prior context: The `parseDurationMs()` parser checks `Number.isFinite()` but not the setTimeout ceiling.
- Why this regressed now: Always present — any user setting `heartbeat.every` to a very large value would hit this.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/config/zod-schema.heartbeat-overflow.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  1. `"99999h"` is rejected with error mentioning setTimeout limit
  2. `"596h"` is accepted (within limit)
  3. `"30m"` is accepted (typical value)
  4. `"abc"` still rejected with exi
items.author: LTS2

items.title: 【提效想法#1】OpenClaw Continuous Learning Hook 系统
items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 最近更新了 continuous-learning-v2 系统，其核心是一个 observe.sh hook：

```bash
# 捕获 PreToolUse/PostToolUse 事件，写入项目级观测数据
HOOK_PHASE=\"${1:-post}\"
INPUT_JSON=$(cat)
```

**关键特性：**
- 项目级观测目录（project-scoped observations）
- 自动检测当前项目 context
- 观测数据包括：tool_use 事件、session 生命周期、checkpoint 进度

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 实现类似的持续学习 hook 系统：

1. 捕获 tool_use 事件（Read/Edit/Exec 等）
2. 写入项目级观测数据到 `~/.openclaw/observations/{project}/`
3. 支持模式识别：识别重复错误、高频操作、提效机会

## 子任务

- [ ] 调研 OpenClaw hook 系统能力
- [ ] 设计观测数据 schema（tool_name, duration, file_context, success）
- [ ] 实现 observe hook 脚本
- [ ] 添加 pattern 分析逻辑（识别重复操作）
- [ ] 与 OpenClaw memory 系统集成

## 启发来源

- everything-claude-code `skills/continuous-learning-v2/hooks/observe.sh`
- `scripts/hooks/session-end-marker.js`
- `scripts/lib/observer-sessions.js`
